summary_text: **Findings (highest severity first)** 1. `High` `x-auto` eval command is now cwd-dependent and fails outside repo root. `scripts/eval/defs/x-auto.yaml` switched to `command: bash scripts/eval/x-auto-health.sh`, which is a relative path, so `skill-eval.sh` run from another cwd returns exit `127` instead of running health check. Refs: [x-auto.yaml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/defs/x-auto.yaml:2), [x-auto-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.sh:1) Observed: `cd /tmp && bash .../scripts/eval/skill-eval.sh --skill x-auto` fails with expected-exit mismatch. 2. `Medium` `install-kernel-launchers` behavior changed in a way that breaks existing expectations and likely existing operator flow. `enumerate_skill_specs` now includes repo `skills/*/SKILL.md` by default, so skill prompts are generated even when no installed local skills exist. Existing regression test now fails on expectation (`skill prompts: none`). Refs: [install-kernel-launchers.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh:247), [install-kernel-launchers.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh:550), [install-kernel-launchers.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh:556) 3. `Medium` local sync migration can silently leave Codex/Claude divergent. `sync-fugue-local-skills.sh` skips existing non-managed targets (default), exits success, and still increments processed counter. In current environment this skips Codex x-auto skills, so “sync complete” does not imply parity. Refs: [sync-fugue-local-skills.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/skills/sync-fugue-local-skills.sh:115), [sync-fugue-local-skills.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/skills/sync-fugue-local-skills.sh:123), [sync-fugue-local-skills.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/skills/sync-fugue-local-skills.sh:175) 4. `Low` health-script precedence may mask runtime-side updates. Wrapper resolves `X_AUTO_HEALTH_SCRIPT` -> repo copy -> `~/.claude` copy. This makes repo copy authoritative without any parity guard, so if home script evolves, eval may unknowingly drift. Refs: [x-auto-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.sh:10), [x-auto-health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py:1) **Verification performed** - Passed: `bash tests/test-codex-skill-launch.sh` - Failed: `bash tests/test-install-kernel-launchers.sh` (fails at expected `skill prompts: none`) - Passed (dry run): `bash scripts/skills/sync-fugue-local-skills.sh --dry-run --target both` - Passed (dry run): `bash scripts/eval/skill-eval.sh --skill x-auto --dry-run` - Failed (runtime from non-repo cwd): `cd /tmp && bash .../scripts/eval/skill-eval.sh --skill x-auto` **Missing verification** - No dedicated tests yet for `scripts/skills/sync-fugue-local-skills.sh`. - No test for `scripts/eval/x-auto-health.sh` resolution order or non-repo-cwd execution path. - Docs were updated, but no migration check confirms first-run parity when existing unmanaged `~/.codex/skills/x-auto-*` directories are present.
